kept.text:
🚨 THIS THING IS DELIVERING FOOD… AND IT LOOKS LIKE A JOKE 🚨

DoorDash just dropped something called the “DoorDash Dot”…

…and people are stopping in the street because it doesn’t make sense.

🤖 What people are actually seeing:

This thing is TINY.

Not “small car” small.
Not “drone” small.

I’m talking…
➡️ looks like a lunchbox
➡️ moves like a toy
➡️ feels like it shouldn’t even work

💥 AND YET, IT’S OUT HERE DOING THIS:

➡️ Driving itself through real streets
➡️ Pulling up to restaurants like a regular customer
➡️ Picking up orders with zero human involved
➡️ Navigating sidewalks, traffic, obstacles… ALL ON ITS OWN
➡️ Showing up at someone’s house with dinner inside

No driver.
No delivery guy.
No interaction.

Just… this little box… handling business.

😳 THIS IS THE PART THAT MESSES WITH YOUR HEAD:

Your brain sees it and goes:
“There’s no way that thing is actually functioning.”

But it is.

Perfectly.

⚠️ This isn’t about food. Not really.

This is:
➡️ AI moving through the real world without supervision
➡️ Machines interacting with businesses like humans
➡️ Automation replacing jobs in ways people aren’t ready for
➡️ Technology shrinking… while getting MORE powerful

📦 WE’VE OFFICIALLY HIT THE POINT WHERE THE FUTURE LOOKS FAKE… BUT IT’S REAL.

And it’s not coming “someday.”

It’s already out there…
rolling up to a restaurant…
grabbing an order…
and pulling away like it owns the place.

🚨 YOU’RE NOT READY FOR HOW FAST THIS IS ABOUT TO SCALE. 🚨

kept.text:
Size manyak bir iş fikriyle geldim ama Türkiye’de yapılır mı, yasal mı bilmiyorum :D

ABD’de insanlar restoran açmadan, herhangi bir yemek yapmadan ayda 30.000 dolar para kazanıyorlar.

Buna ghost kitchen demişler. 
Türkçesi hayalet mutfak. 

DoorDash ve Uber Eats gibi uygulamalar, herkesin bir saatten kısa sürede platforma restoran eklemesine izin veriyor.

Basit mantığı var.

Nashville Hot Chicken Co gibi bir isim seçiyorsun, gerçek bir restoranın menüsünü internetten kopyalıyorsun ve her ürüne %40 zam koyuyorsun.

Ortada gerçek bir mutfak yok :D

Dropshipping mantığına benzer aslında. 

22 dolarlık bir sandviç siparişi geldiğinde, ikinci bir telefondan DoorDash açıp aynı sandviçi 3 blok ötede gerçek bir restorandan 14 dolara sipariş ediyorsun.

Teslimat adresini müşterinin evi olarak değiştiriyorsun ve kurye, yemeği senin hayalet mutfağından geldiğini sanarak teslim ediyor.

Sen de tek bir tavuk parçasına bile dokunmadan 8 dolar kar ediyorsun.

10 tane sahte restoranı aynı anda çalıştırırsan, her biri farklı mutfak türünde, ayda 30.000 dolar kazanabiliyorsun. 

Hiçbir şeye sahibi olmadan.

Bazıları işi daha da büyütmek için UPS posta kutusu kiralayıp bunu adres olarak kullanıyor, böylece listeleme doğrulamasını geçiyor.

Bazıları ise aynı zip code içinde 30–40 sahte markadan oluşan ağlar kurup hepsini aynı 2–3 gerçek restorana bağlıyor.

Müşteri hiçbir şey fark etmiyor. 

Sandviç sipariş ediyor, yemeğini alıyor ve var olmayan bir işletmeye 5 yıldız veriyor.

Gerçek restoran aslında var olmayan bir rakip için fazla mesai yapıyor.

platformların çoğu mutfağın gerçekten var olup olmadığını kontrol etmiyor.

En saçma tarafı bunun çoğunun teknik olarak yasal olması.

Yemek yeniden satılabiliyor. Ürünlere fiyat eklemek serbest. Platforma adresi olan bir işletme listelemek mümkün.

Sınırı aştığın tek yer sahte isim ve sahte görseller kullanmak. Bu yanlış beyan sayılabilir ama platformlar bugüne kadar bunun için pek dava açmamıș. 

En kötü ihtimalle hesabın kapatılır, ertesi gün başka bir LLC ile yeniden açarsın.

kept.text:
Let me talk about instant gratification and why it's destroying retail investors in $ASTS and honestly in life in general.

We live in a world where people can't wait for anything. Amazon same day delivery. DoorDash in 30 minutes. TikTok in 15 second clips. Swipe left, swipe right. Next. Next. Next. And people have taken that same energy into investing and it is genuinely one of the most destructive things I've ever watched happen in real time.

Here's what's wild to me. AST SpaceMobile went from $2 to $130. Let that sink in. $2 to $130. And now it's sitting at $74 and people are acting like the company is failing. You bought at $2 and you're complaining at $74? That's a 37x return and you're on X posting about how management lies and misses timelines? Be serious.

But let's talk about those timelines because this is where people really expose how little they understand about engineering, manufacturing, and what it actually means to build something that has never existed before.

AST SpaceMobile is not building an app. They're not dropping a software update. They are hand-building the most complex commercial satellites ever put into low earth orbit. Satellites so large, so technically advanced, that when you look at the BlueBird specs compared to anything else up there, it genuinely looks like science fiction. These things are pushing the absolute boundaries of what human engineering can do right now. Abel Avellan has been sleeping on the production floor. Let that image sit with you for a second. The CEO. Sleeping on the floor of the factory. Because that's the level of commitment happening behind the scenes while retail investors are posting bear takes from their couch.

And yes. They've missed timelines. You know who else missed timelines? Every single space company in the history of space. Every single one.

Elon Musk said people would be on Mars by 2025. It's 2026. Not even close. He's now saying 2030, which he'll probably miss too. SpaceX has missed more deadlines than I can count. Starlink missed timelines. Falcon Heavy missed timelines. Starship has been "almost ready" for years. And what's SpaceX worth right now? They're targeting a $2 TRILLION IPO valuation. TWO TRILLION DOLLARS. So the next time someone tells you ASTS missed a launch window so it's over, remind them that the greatest space company ever built missed virtually every major deadline they ever set and they're worth $2 trillion.

Amazon's Kuiper? Late. Blue Origin? Chronically late. New Glenn just put a BlueBird satellite in the wrong orbit. These are the best funded aerospace organizations on planet Earth and they can't hit a deadline. That's not failure. That's the nature of operating at the edge of what's physically possible.

What AST has accomplished is actually extraordinary when you look at it objectively. They went from a startup nobody believed in to having AT&T, Verizon, Rakuten, Orange, Telefonica, TELUS and 50+ of the world's largest mobile operators signed as partners serving nearly 6 billion subscribers. They got FCC approval for a 248 satellite constellation. They secured prime contractor status on a $151 billion Golden Dome defense program. They have $2B+ in cash, $1.2B in contracted commitments, and just filed a patent that could multiply their spectrum capacity by 3x to 10x making their already extraordinary spectrum position worth potentially $6.8B to $17B in added value in the US alone. And they did all of this while building something that has literally never been built before.

But the bears and the impatient want results now. Today. This quarter. And if they don't get it this quarter they're done. Moving on. Next.

That's not investing. That's gambling with an impatience problem layered on top.

Real investing requires understanding what you own, having conviction in the vision, and having the patience to let world-changing companies actually change the world. It took Amazon 10 years to be taken seriously. Netflix was a joke for years. Tesla was the most shorted stock on earth before it went up 10,000%. The pattern is always the same. Visionary company. Massive execution challenges in uncharted territory. Impatient retail investors exit. Institutions accumulate. Company delivers. Stock re-rates violently upward. Retail watches from the sideline.

Don't be that retail investor.

If you don't understand engineering timelines, if you've never built anything complex in your life, if you've never managed a manufacturing process for something that's never been made before, maybe pump the brakes before you post your bear take about a company whose CEO is sleeping on the factory floor to make sure a $30 million satellite is perfect before it goes to space.

Because once it goes up, it's up. Forever. You don't get to patch it. You don't get to recall it. You have one shot. And that's exactly why they take the time they take. And that's exactly why the bears will be wrong.

Patience isn't weakness. In investing, patience is the entire game.

$ASTS 🛰️

NFA

quoted_tweet.text:
@Polymarket This is extremely old news, and they have their dates incorrect. This initiative seems to recirculate every 8 months when the news cycle is slow.

We announced this initiative around 2020. Hundreds if not thousands of locations have already had their lobby drink stations removed, and it’s not a big deal. We announced this about 15 years prior to the target date.

All new McDonald’s built in the last 3 years have this “new” setup. All remodels have had the option for the same setup over the past 4 years.

No rationale was given. This isn’t a cost move, as it will cost me $30k to make the change (and I still offer free refills). I can speculate that with digital orders now making up a much bigger part of our business (+30%), it’s much easier to have the fountain behind the counter to fulfill digital and delivery orders. They announced this change around 2020, and it’s been a pull, not a push. I feel they are simply preparing their setup for what they think our business will need in 2036.

At first I was very apprehensive when it was originally announced (and reported on 5 years ago), but now it completely makes sense with customer flow.

We handle hundreds of delivery orders a day. Having to fill drinks in the lobby or interrupt the drive-thru “robot” is neither fast nor efficient.

Specifically, the consumer habit of not eating at McDonalds.

When most McDonalds customers ate inside the building, the hassle of maintaining a publicly accessible soda fountain was paid for by extra orders.

Most McDonalds orders are either drivethru or doordash now.

kept.text:
B2C markets start as duopolies.

But nearly always end with one dominant winner.

Uber now 30x Lyft. Doordash now 100x Grubhub. Tinder now 10x Bumble. Spotify now 10x Pandora. Coinbase 100x Gemini. 

Polymarket v Kalshi is 1:1 today but I promise you it will not end like that.

tweet.text:
One of the best trades this cycle has been the privacy coin rotation sequence.

When $ZEC confirms a breakout on its chart, $DASH and $ZEN usually follow with violent moves within days.

We’ve seen it repeatedly over the last 12 months, and it just happened again. 

Just today, those coins are up +40% and +22% respectively.

$XMR has been the slower laggard, but even then, it typically finds its breakout within days to weeks.

Remember. Future alt seasons will be driven by narratives.

And understanding narrative sequencing is one of the most powerful edges you can develop in this market.
